title: Investigate how substrate enforces 'agents never operate outside a workflow envelope'
description: Gap 2 from the workflow-schema-universal-landscape investigation. The agent-blame-shielding doctrine claims 'agents never operate outside a workflow envelope.' Substrate has the typed workflow contract, but agent-side RUNTIME enforcement is implicit. Investigation scope: (1) does substrate's harness layer (harness-header-schemas, ai-harness-schemas) already have a workflow-envelope check? Read the existing harness schemas; (2) does the runtime (clia-agent-cli, swift-harness-cli, openclaw) bind agent operations to WorkflowModel instances at invocation time? Trace what happens when a skill is invoked; (3) is there a typed AgentExecutionContextRequirement primitive we'd need, or is this purely runtime discipline? (4) author findings in investigation.docc + recommend whether new typed primitives are needed OR whether agent-runtime lint checks are sufficient. Important: this might surface as a HARNESS doctrine, not a schema-universal one — the answer could be 'every harness must validate WorkflowModel binding before executing agent ops' as harness-layer policy.
